profile.name: Tobi Awolaju
profile.title: Systems Engineer (Web3 Infra | AI Agents)
profile.bio:
"Bridging the gap between Software and On-Chain Logic."

What I Bring to a Team:
• Real-time Web3 Gaming: Building 20Hz WebSockets and syncing complex 3D game states to on-chain execution.
• Internal Tooling & Automation: If the team does a task twice, I build a local AI agent or a CLI tool to automate it.

writeup.goal: Build a resilient EVM indexer that never drops data, even when the blockchain reorganizes.
writeup.hardPart: Public RPCs are unreliable. I built a multi-node ingestion engine that cross-references 3 different Monad nodes, with a custom 'ReorgManager' that detects parent-hash mismatches and automatically rolls back bad state.
writeup.stack: Node.js, Express, Svelte, Firebase RTDB, EVM RPCs.

projects.title: World of Nads ↗
projects.description: What happens when you bridge high-speed gaming and Monad? I built this entire world—from the 3D assets to the 20Hz WebSocket server.

writeup.overview: A solo experiment in end-to-end creation: 3D modeling, smart contracts, and real-time multiplayer architecture.
features: Low-friction Web3 onboarding via embedded wallets
writeup.architecture: Godot → WASM + React, Node.js WebSocket server @20Hz, Monad Smart Contracts.
writeup.infrastructure: High-performance deployment on Vercel and Render.
writeup.techStack: Godot, TypeScript, Solidity, Monad, Node.js, PostgreSQL.
writeup.web3: Real-time state sync coupled with Privy for seamless identity and on-chain payouts.
writeup.challenges: Solving the 'impossible' task of keeping 20 players perfectly synced while handling blockchain state.
writeup.outcomes: A live, functional product used by real people, proving that a solo builder can ship a complete Web3 ecosystem.
writeup.future: Expanding the physical laws of the game and deeper Monad integration.

projects.title: DominusQuant: Monad Keeper & Algo-Trading Bot ↗
projects.description: Automated trading infrastructure leveraging Monad's gas pricing logs and distributed behavioral signals for healthy, non-toxic execution.

writeup.goal: Automate complex DeFi strategies (like liquidations, statistical arbitrage, and tokenomic rebalancing) on a high-speed chain.
writeup.hardPart: Monad's architecture is designed to prevent traditional mempool sniping and sandwich attacks. To find an edge, I had to build a system that parses confirmed on-chain state, gas pricing logs, and AI-aggregated behavioral signals in real-time to execute trades strictly based on speed and statistical advantage.
writeup.stack: TypeScript, Monad Situation Dashboard APIs, Distributed Node.js Microservices, Firebase.

projects.title: Noogler - Embodied AI ↗
projects.description: Giving the ghost a body. An AI agent that doesn't just call APIs, but actually sees the screen and moves the mouse like a human.

writeup.overview: An obsession with 'Digital Agency': building an agent that can operate software autonomously by observing and interacting.
features: Real-time screen capture and visual reasoning
Gemini 2.0 Live API for high-level decision making
Rust-based local agent for direct OS-level control
writeup.architecture: Node.js Cloud Backend + Gemini 2.0, Svelte UI, and a local Rust agent for automation.
writeup.infrastructure: Local automation layer optimized for Windows environments.
writeup.challenges: Ensuring the agent can handle errors and navigate diverse software interfaces autonomously.
writeup.outcomes: A working prototype that can fix its own errors in IDEs, Excel, and browsers.
writeup.future: Advancing toward a fully autonomous companion that learns from user behavior.

projects.title: ProofForge ↗
projects.description: Tamper-evident development timelines. Every Git commit, anchored on-chain.

writeup.goal: Make build history unforgeable. Prove what was built, when, and by whom — without trusting GitHub, a repo host, or any single party.
writeup.hardPart: The trust problem is subtle. GitHub repos can be force-pushed. History can be rewritten. The only moment you can't fake is the HMAC-signed webhook payload — that's when GitHub itself attests a push happened. I built the entire anchoring system around that specific event, not around repo state. The Monad contract only ever sees payloads that passed HMAC-SHA256 verification. That's the difference between 'tamper-resistant' and actually tamper-evident.
writeup.stack: Node.js, Solidity, Monad Testnet, GitHub Webhooks, HMAC-SHA256.

projects.title: HARE — Headless Autonomous Relay Engine ↗
projects.description: Agent infrastructure that removes the human from execution and quality control. Both loops.

writeup.goal: Build the foundation for agents that are autonomous in the way that actually matters — no human reviewing outputs, no human retrying failures, no human deciding if the result was good enough.
writeup.hardPart: Most 'autonomous' agent frameworks are autonomous in name only. They remove the human from execution but quietly keep them in the quality control loop. HARE treats reliability as a first-class architectural constraint. Tasks must self-evaluate against a success condition, self-retry with adjusted context on failure, and emit structured results that downstream systems can trust without human inspection.
writeup.stack: Node.js, TypeScript, LLM APIs, Webhook Infrastructure.
